task_completion.description: Agent successfully logged into the NOSH ChartingSystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH dashboard or main interface is visible in the web browser, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the chart for the correct patient, Walter Bishop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active patient context or header displays 'Walter Bishop' (DOB: 01/20/1948, PID: 9999).

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct subject for the letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A subject or title input field in the letter/message creation form contains exactly 'Insurance Claim Denial - Action Required'.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct content for the letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area for the letter body contains the specified text: 'Your claim for the visit on 01/15/2026 was denied by the payer due to missing coordination of benefits information. Please contact your insurance provider immediately to update your status.'

task_completion.description: Agent saved or submitted the letter to the patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification (e.g., 'Letter saved', 'Message sent') is visible, or the newly created letter appears in the patient's chart history, documents, or communications list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the NOSH graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or direct database inserts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the NOSH UI. There should be no terminal windows executing SQL commands, curl requests, or API scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent entered the letter details into the actual NOSH application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text fields being filled (subject and body) are clearly part of the NOSH web application interface, not a separate text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the successful creation of the letter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are not open and being used to alter the DOM to display fake success messages or chart entries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: Walter Bishop (DOB: 01/20/1948, PID: 9999). Expected Subject: 'Insurance Claim Denial - Action Required'. Expected Content must include references to the visit on 01/15/2026 and 'missing coordination of benefits'. The system being used is NOSH ChartingSystem.
